title: MLX Documentation
description: Apple's official MLX documentation explains the Apple-silicon-first model: unified memory, lazy evaluation, multi-device execution, and LLM examples.

title: llama.cpp README
description: The most cited primary source for GGUF-based local inference, Apple Metal support, quantization tradeoffs, and OpenAI-compatible local serving.

title: Ollama macOS Docs
description: Official guidance for installing and running Ollama on macOS, including the local runtime and service behavior developers build on top of.

title: Ollama Tool Calling Docs
description: Official docs for tool calling, structured arguments, and the multi-turn orchestration pattern that turns local chat into agent behavior.

title: Aider with Ollama
description: A respected practical guide to using local models for real coding workflows, including context-window expectations and model-selection caveats.

title: Compose File Reference
description: Docker's canonical Compose specification for defining services, networks, volumes, restart behavior, and multi-container applications.

title: Docker Storage Guide
description: Official Docker guidance on writable layers, named volumes, bind mounts, and when each persistence choice is appropriate.

title: Restic Documentation
description: The official restic documentation covers repositories, backup commands, retention policy, integrity checks, and restore procedures.

title: Tailscale SSH
description: Official Tailscale docs on identity-based SSH, access controls, and safer remote administration without exposing a public port.

title: Tailscale Serve
description: Official guidance on sharing local services privately within a tailnet, a strong pattern for homelabs that should not be internet-public.

title: What Makes a Good Game Mechanism?
description: Jamey Stemaier's design article on the qualities of strong mechanisms, especially meaningful choices, intuitive structure, and tension.

title: The Workshopping Skill
description: Daniel Cook's respected design essay on constraints, iteration, ego-detachment, and generating multiple solutions to the same design problem.

title: How to Playtest like a Boss
description: Board Game Design Lab's in-depth playtesting guide, focused on turning sessions into useful evidence instead of vague opinions.

title: Refining Your Game
description: A practical League of Gamemakers piece on staged testing, blind playtests, and how to treat harsh sessions as design data instead of personal failure.

title: Writing Rules
description: Board Game Design Lab's rules-writing hub on organization, terminology, examples, and teaching through the rulebook instead of designer presence.

title: John Gruber's Markdown Syntax
description: The original Markdown syntax guide and philosophy statement, still the canonical source for readability-first Markdown writing.

title: CommonMark Spec
description: The current CommonMark specification, with detailed examples that clarify ambiguous behaviors across renderers.

title: CommonMark Tutorial
description: A learner-friendly interactive tutorial that turns the CommonMark rules into short practice lessons and examples.

title: GitHub Flavored Markdown Spec
description: GitHub's formal Markdown dialect spec covering tables, task lists, strikethrough, autolinks, and other conventions encountered in modern workflows.

title: MDN Markdown Writing Guide
description: A high-quality editorial example of using Markdown in real documentation work, including practical style choices and authoring habits.

title: Pro Git
description: The canonical free Git book by Scott Chacon and Ben Straub. It is still one of the best primary sources for understanding Git's model, commands, branching, remotes, and history.

title: Adding locally hosted code to GitHub
description: GitHub's official guide for turning an existing local project into a GitHub repository from the command line.

title: About remote repositories
description: GitHub's explanation of remotes, remote URLs, and how local Git repositories connect to GitHub.

title: Generating a new SSH key and adding it to the ssh-agent
description: GitHub's official SSH setup guide for creating keys, loading them into your agent, and using SSH with GitHub more comfortably.

title: Working with forks
description: GitHub's official guide to what forks are, how they relate to upstream repositories, and when they make sense in real Git workflows.

title: Adding an existing project to GitHub using GitHub Desktop
description: Official GitHub Desktop documentation for publishing an existing local repository through the desktop client.

title: Adding a file to a repository
description: GitHub's web UI guide for adding files to a repository, useful as a contrast to a full Git-based workflow.

title: Git and GitHub for Beginners - Crash Course
description: A widely respected beginner-friendly video from freeCodeCamp that walks through Git basics, GitHub, and core command-line workflows.

title: Git and GitHub Tutorial for Beginners
description: A practical beginner video by Kevin Stratvert that covers Git fundamentals, GitHub setup, and a command-line-first workflow clearly.
